from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    Handles single‑triple inputs with more natural templates.
    """
    # Simple template map for special predicates that need subject‑first phrasing.
    special_templates = {
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}.",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"One of the ethnic groups in {s} is {o}.",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}.",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}.",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} rises to {o}.",
        "architect": lambda s, o: f"{s} was designed by {o}.",
        "buildingType": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o}.",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was completed in {o}.",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was built in {o}.",
    }

    # Generic verb map for remaining predicates.
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has the postcode area",
    }

    # If there is only one triple, use a special template if available.
    if len(triples) == 1:
        t = triples[0]
        if t.predicate in special_templates:
            return special_templates[t.predicate](t.subject, t.object).strip()
        # Fallback generic handling
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, "has")
        clause = f"{t.subject} {verb} {t.object}."
        return clause[0].upper() + clause[1:]

    # Group triples by subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        clauses = []
        # Preferred ordering for readability.
        preferred = ["location", "height", "architect", "buildingType", "completionDate"]
        handled = set()

        # Helper to add clause for a predicate.
        def add_clause(pred):
            for tr in group:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    if pred in special_templates:
                        # Use the special template but omit the subject at start.
                        clause = special_templates[pred](tr.subject, tr.object)
                        # Remove leading subject if present.
                        clause = clause.replace(f"{tr.subject} ", "")
                    else:
                        verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                        # Adjust count nouns.
                        if pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                            clause = f"{verb} {tr.object} floors"
                        else:
                            clause = f"{verb} {tr.object}"
                    clauses.append(clause.rstrip('.'))
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        for pred in preferred:
            add_clause(pred)

        # Remaining predicates.
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            if tr.predicate in special_templates:
                clause = special_templates[tr.predicate](tr.subject, tr.object)
                clause = clause.replace(f"{tr.subject} ", "")
            else:
                verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
                if tr.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                    clause = f"{verb} {tr.object} floors"
                else:
                    clause = f"{verb} {tr.object}"
            clauses.append(clause.rstrip('.'))

        # Combine clauses into one sentence.
        sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(clauses) + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    return final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:] if final_text else ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
